metadata.title: CHSH Angle Explorer — Why 45° Wins

beats.narration_text: Talofa. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of chsh angle explorer. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of chsh angle explorer

beats.narration_text: CHSH Angle Explorer is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. E(θ_a,θ_b) = cos(θ_a−θ_b) for |Φ+⟩. Drag four pointers on the polar dial — find why 45° spacing is the magic configuration.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: CHSH Angle Explorer

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of CHSH Angle Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: ${k}. Verify: E(θ_a,θ_b) = cos(θ_a−θ_b) for |Φ+⟩. Drag four pointers on the polar dial — find why 45° spacing is the magic configurati. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of CHSH Angle Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: ${k}. Verify: E(θ_a,θ_b) = cos(θ_a−θ_b) for |Φ+⟩. D

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from vol4-chsh-angle-explorer.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from vol4-chsh-angle-explorer.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: chsh angle explorer. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag ${k} from 0 to 360. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of ${k} from 0 to 360

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of ${k} from 0 to 360

beats.narration_text: E(θ_a,θ_b) = cos(θ_a−θ_b) for |Φ+⟩. Drag four pointers on the polar dial — find why 45° spacing is the magic configuration.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of chsh angle explorer. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this CHSH Angle Explorer simulation and add a second slider that controls ${k}. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this CHSH Angle Explorer simulation and add a second slider that controls ${k}. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
